source_note: PILOT build. Sourced from the S&P Money Manager Database Manager export (results (3).xls), which records, per plan, which external manager runs how much of that plan's assets, in what asset type/style. Segment routing reuses the same category/plan-type rules as every other Atlas data product. 'Self-managed' and 'Not disclosed' are kept as two separate categories, not merged: 'Self-managed' is only used where S&P explicitly tags the mandate as run in-house (e.g. 'In House Asset Management') -- covering both rows with a specific dollar figure and, for a plan whose only manager tag anywhere is that self-managed tag, the plan's full remaining gap too (S&P is telling us the whole plan is run in-house, just not always the exact split). 'Not disclosed' is reserved for genuine unknowns -- plans with no manager information at all, or plans naming at least one real external manager where a leftover gap remains that can't be attributed to anyone specifically. Neither bucket has an asset-type breakdown, since the raw data doesn't say how those amounts are invested. 'identified_coverage_pct' on each segment counts both named external managers and self-managed amounts as identified -- only 'Not disclosed' counts against coverage. 7 of 2193 plans ($1,093.01bn) could not be routed to any segment and are excluded entirely -- same categories excluded everywhere else in Atlas (Reserve Banks and similar non-pension pools). Manager names are grouped by the firm name portion of the raw 'Name, City, Country' field, so the same manager's different regional offices roll up together. Never cross-checked against the Manager file for any other country yet -- this is a single-country pilot.
